input: How much PTO do I get?
expected_behavior: Agent should ask about employment classification (full-time vs part-time) and/or tenure since PTO accrual is tiered: 0-2 yrs 15 days; 3-5 yrs 20 days; 6-9 yrs 25 days; 10+ yrs 30 days.
expected_facts: Should ask about full-time or part-time|Should ask about tenure or length of employment
not_expected: A specific PTO number without first clarifying classification and tenure

expected_behavior: Agent should ask about job level since bonus targets vary: L2-L3 5%-10%; L4-L5 10%-20%; L6+ 20%-35% of base salary.
expected_facts: Should ask about job level or grade
not_expected: A specific bonus percentage without clarifying level

input: Am I eligible for parental leave?
expected_behavior: Agent should ask whether the employee is the primary or secondary caregiver (16 weeks vs 6 weeks) and whether they have 6 months continuous employment.
expected_facts: Should ask about primary or secondary caregiver|May ask about length of employment
not_expected: Stating eligibility without asking about caregiver status

input: What leave am I entitled to?
expected_behavior: Agent should ask about location (US vs UK policies differ significantly) and possibly employment type before answering.
expected_facts: Should ask about location or region|US and UK leave policies differ significantly
not_expected: Dumping all leave policies without scoping to the user

input: How much notice do I need to give?
expected_behavior: Agent should ask what the notice is for — PTO requires 2 weeks; parental leave requires 30 days; the context is ambiguous.
expected_facts: Should ask what type of notice or leave the user is referring to
not_expected: Assuming a specific notice type without clarification

expected_behavior: Agent should ask about employment classification and hours since full-time gets benefits on first day of month after hire and part-time needs 20+ hours/week.
expected_facts: Should ask about employment status (full-time or part-time)|May ask about weekly hours

expected_behavior: Agent should ask about performance rating since merit increases vary: rating 5 gets 4.5%-6.0%; rating 4 gets 2.5%-4.0%; rating 3 gets 1.0%-2.5%; ratings 1-2 get 0%.
expected_facts: Should ask about performance rating or review score
not_expected: A specific merit percentage without knowing the rating

input: Can you summarize all the types of leave I'm eligible for as a full-time employee in our New York office?
expected_behavior: Agent should answer directly since the user specified full-time and US location. Should NOT ask redundant clarifying questions. Should provide US leave types from HR-POL-006.
expected_facts: Should answer directly with US leave types|PTO accrual tiered by tenure|10 sick days|Parental leave|FMLA|Bereavement|Jury duty|Military leave
not_expected: Asking about employment classification (already stated as full-time)|Asking about location (already stated as New York)
